summary: Multi-generational astronomical observation register spanning ~162 solar years (circa 1350-1512 CE) from Incahuasi. 322 cords in a 26x9 matrix where each row = observation period (~6.2 years) and each column = one of 9 celestial phenomena. All 9 columns strongly intercorrelated (r=0.5-0.86) = sky visibility fingerprint. P1 (Moon): lag-3 autocorrelation r=0.43 = 18.6yr lunar nodal cycle. P7 (Mars): lag-1 r=0.60 = 3 synodic cycles. P8 (Eclipses): highest CV (0.59), strongest decline. Only qaqa (dark constellations) as text labels. The longest continuous astronomical record in the pre-Columbian Americas. 12 STRING, 83% coverage.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  322 cords (L1: 298, L2: 15, L3: 9). Architecture: 0-SEUL, tabular 26x9.
  3-color system: W(143), AB(124), NB(47), MB(7).
  37 clusters (26 regular 9-cord + 11 framing/summary).
  Excavation: Sector A, Subsector 1, UA 4, Unidad 20N, UE 16.
  12 STRING (83% hits — all qaqa variants), 226 INT, 30 EMPTY, 54 L-1 (17%).

THE 26x9 MATRIX
  26 regular sections of 9 cords each. Grand total: 59,166. 59,166/365.25 = 162.0 solar years.
  Peak: Section 12 (sum=4273, AB color). Lowest: Section 32 (sum=420, W color).
  Color shift: AB dominates early sections (1-13), W dominates later (22-37) = scribe change or era change.

INTER-COLUMN CORRELATIONS (DECISIVE EVIDENCE)
  All 9 columns strongly intercorrelated: P1-P2 r=0.829, P6-P7 r=0.860, P5-P9 r=0.805.
  Statistical fingerprint of astronomical register: when sky is clear, ALL observations increase together.
  Accounting khipus show INDEPENDENT or NEGATIVE columns (cf. UR270 r=-0.636).

TEMPORAL TRENDS
  P5 (Jupiter): rho=-0.456, p=0.019 (DECLINING)
  P7 (Mars): rho=-0.496, p=0.010 (DECLINING)
  P8 (Eclipses): rho=-0.525, p=0.006 (DECLINING)
  Decreasing observational activity in later periods = political disruption (Inca conquest ~1470?) or scribe succession.

COLUMN-TO-OBJECT MAPPING
  P1 = Moon (mama): lag-3 r=0.43 = 18.6yr lunar nodal cycle. 2nd highest sum.
  P2 = Venus (paka): highest sum + lowest CV (0.27) = most stable, most observed.
  P3 = Dark constellations (qaqa): lower count (moonless nights only). The ONLY text label.
  P4 = Saturn (taki?): synodic 378.1d.
  P5 = Jupiter: synodic 398.9d, significant decline.
  P6 = Pleiades (kaki): 3rd highest, visible 10/12 months.
  P7 = Mars (kama): lag-1 r=0.60 = 3 synodic cycles (3x2.14=6.4yr). High CV = eccentric orbit.
  P8 = Eclipses (maqa): lowest sum + highest CV (0.59) + strongest decline = rare, unpredictable.
  P9 = Scorpio (chaki): Chaki T'aklla, visible ~8 months at Canete latitude.

VOCABULARY
  qaqa (rock/dark constellation x6), qaqaqaqa (double label x4), qaqaqa (variant x2).
  Only qaqa variants — this is a numerical register with occasional text labels.

CROSS-CORPUS
  Same 9-column format as UR006 (Leymebamba, June 1473) and UR1145 (Pachacamac, 1481-1533).
  Found in same storage unit as UR270 (peanut accounting) — demonstrates astronomical and administrative records coexisted at Incahuasi.
  The longest continuous astronomical record in the pre-Columbian Americas.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — 162-year astronomical register, column-object mapping, correlation analysis
Urton & Chu 2013 — Incahuasi excavation, Sector A
Urton 1981, At the Crossroads of the Earth and the Sky — dark constellations
Incahuasi — Inca military base, Canete Valley, southern coast
